releases.title: Attention, on your terms
changes: Notify, Glance, and Quiet now provide one coherent policy for completion openings, sounds, urgent task states, and inspectable unread signals.
Local completed tasks can show a bounded one-line outcome from Codex’s final response, with a dedicated display setting and no remote model-output transfer.
Active tasks now show sanitized project and branch identity while nested subagents roll into one parent row and elevate anything that needs you.
Codex account usage now follows the actual primary and secondary window durations, labels each window, and states when a limit is reached.
Quiet mode remains visible in the open notch, and the attention selector supports keyboard, pointer, and assistive-technology use without losing focus.

releases.title: Back where the notch belongs
changes: Restored Codex Notch above the menu bar so it once again reads as an extension of the physical notch.
The overlay keeps its new virtual-desktop behavior without falling back beneath menu-bar items.

releases.title: The notch follows your workspace
changes: Codex Notch now remains available as you move between virtual desktops on the same display.
Pressing Control–Shift–H on another desktop now reveals the notch there instead of closing an unseen window elsewhere.

releases.title: A first run that earns its finish
changes: A new four-step onboarding introduces the notch, installs the local completion hook, teaches the global shortcut, and ends with a clear first-success moment.
Setup now verifies the real Codex hook installation and approval state instead of asking you to confirm trust manually.
Replay the complete onboarding any time from the This Mac card in Connections settings.
Onboarding motion is brief, topologically consistent, and replaced by gentle fades when Reduce Motion is enabled.

releases.title: Your weekly limit, actually live
changes: Weekly usage now reads directly from the running Codex app-server connection, with executable discovery retained as a fallback.
Opening or refreshing Codex Notch no longer replaces a known percentage or useful failure message with indefinite loading dots.
Fallback checks now run without blocking live usage updates, share a bounded time budget, and avoid retrying the same executable through multiple symlinks.

releases.title: One task, then the whole picture
changes: Stop-hook openings now show only the task that caused Codex Notch to appear.
Hover over that compact notification or press Control–Shift–H to reveal the full task list without losing your place.
The compact notch now grows into the full view with a top-anchored, critically damped transition, with a gentle fade when Reduce Motion is enabled.

releases.title: Built to stay consistent
changes: Settings pages now render as independent components, reducing the risk that one tab can distort another tab’s layout.
Task highlighting, relative ages, shortcut freezing, weekly usage, and host status now pass through isolated notch components without changing their behavior.
New release checks block oversized Swift files before they can turn back into hidden UI and test monoliths.

releases.title: Know what just finished
changes: Stop-hook openings now softly highlight the exact task that brought Codex Notch into view.
Completed tasks now show a compact relative age that stays current while the notch remains open.

releases.title: Your limit, at a glance
changes: The weekly-limit badge now shows only the remaining percentage, keeping the value readable in the compact header.
Hovering the percentage now clearly explains that it is your account-wide weekly Codex limit, not task context.

releases.title: Your weekly limit, unmistakably
changes: The header now identifies the account-wide seven-day Codex limit as 7d, so it cannot be mistaken for task context remaining.
Codex Notch now rejects context-specific fallback data and only uses the main Codex account bucket for weekly usage.

releases.title: The signal, not the noise
changes: Weekly usage now lives as a compact percentage in the header, with forecast, reset, and recent-trend details on hover.
Host health now stays compact as a colored count; hover still shows every machine and click still opens Connections.
Removed stray Button text from custom controls and stopped status refreshes from rebuilding or resizing the open notch.

releases.title: Every host, every signal
changes: Weekly usage now stays visible while loading and explains failures instead of silently disappearing.
Click an unavailable weekly-usage card to retry, including when Codex is installed in your personal Applications folder.
The host badge now counts this Mac and every configured remote, shows each host on hover, and opens Connections when clicked.
Removed the redundant active and completed totals from the notch header.

releases.title: Frozen where it belongs
changes: The frozen-view status now appears beside Active tasks instead of replacing the Codex Notch shortcut.
Hover over Frozen for a concise explanation of why live updates pause and how to resume them.

releases.title: Workspaces with clear ownership
changes: Made the macOS app a self-contained Swift package inside its app workspace.
Separated Ubuntu publisher tests from repository release-tool tests.
Kept local checks, CI, signed builds, and release artifacts on one root orchestration path.

releases.title: A sturdier foundation
changes: Separated the macOS app and Ubuntu companion into explicit app directories so each platform's shipped files stay together.
Unified local checks, CI, installers, and signed builds behind the same repository paths and commands.
Preserved all existing task, theme, sound, usage, changelog, and remote-host behavior during the reorganization.

releases.title: A changelog that opens safely
changes: Fixed the crash when opening Changelog in the signed app.
Added a packaged-app smoke check so a missing or misplaced changelog blocks future builds.
Pressing Control and Shift during a finished-task preview now keeps Codex Notch open.

releases.title: A changelog you can trust
changes: Added a Changelog tab to Settings with a scrollable history of shipped improvements.
Bundled release notes now power both the app and each GitHub Release.
Added CI and release validation so a version cannot ship without matching changelog notes.

releases.title: Open from the notch
changes: Hover over the physical notch or top-center screen area to open Codex Notch with a smooth expansion.
Added intentional-hover detection that avoids repeated or accidental openings.

releases.title: At home in the menu bar
changes: Moved the notch header into the menu-bar band.
Header controls now flank the measured hardware notch instead of sitting below it.

releases.title: Shortcuts stay put
changes: Holding Control and Shift now freezes task ordering so an incoming update cannot change the shortcut under your fingers.
Added a visible LOCKED state while shortcut ordering is frozen.

releases.title: Know your weekly pace
changes: Added weekly Codex usage remaining directly to the notch.
Added local usage history and a trend-based forecast for when the weekly limit may run out.
Connections is now the default Settings tab.

releases.title: Task settings, restored
changes: Rebuilt the Tasks tab layout so controls, descriptions, and shortcuts remain readable without overlap.

releases.title: Themes with a point of view
changes: Added authored themes with distinct type and surface treatments, including an all-black theme.

releases.title: Active tasks at your fingertips
changes: Active tasks now participate in the same keyboard shortcut flow as completed tasks.

releases.title: Sounds that ship
changes: Fixed packaged completion sounds and sound preview crashes.
Theme previews now use the real Codex Notch instead of an embedded imitation.

releases.title: Settings that hold their shape
changes: Kept the Settings window at a stable size while switching between tabs.

releases.title: Choices you can actually see
changes: Fixed collapsed theme and sound grids and added layout regression coverage.

releases.title: The customizable Codex Notch
changes: Added active tasks, themes, completion sounds, remote-host health, and shortcut hints.
Added Codex Notch Do Not Disturb for suppressing automatic openings without using macOS Focus.
